channel.title: The Mutapa Times — Zimbabwe Stories
channel.description: Packed Instagram-Story autolist — business, property, jobs and internships in one feed. Each item ships with a 1080×1920 story card and a 'Read more on Mutapa Times' CTA.

item.title: Zimbabwe in strong position after first day’s play against Bangladesh @Reuters
item.description: via Reuters #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Scottland FC Eyes Mamelodi Sundowns Striker
item.description: Scottland FC targets a Mamelodi Sundowns striker in a potential blockbuster transfer that could reshape their attack ahead of the new season. The post Scottland FC Eyes Mamelodi Sundowns Striker appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Zimbabwean table R4.1m bid for Sundowns striker Peter Shalulile @bulawayo24
item.description: Scottland FC are preparing an ambitious move to sign Namibian striker Peter Shalulile after tabling a reported R4.1 million (US$250,000) signing-on package as they strengthen their squad for next seas... via Bulawayo24 News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: WHERE TO WATCH: Zimbabwe v Bangladesh Only Test 2026 @3menonaboat
item.description:
Broadcast details for Zimbabwe v Bangladesh (Only Test)

The post WHERE TO WATCH: Zimbabwe v Bangladesh Only Test 2026 first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

via Three Men On Boat #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Three Men On Boat

item.title: Zimbabwe opt to bowl; Bangladesh pick Amite Hasan over Mahidul Islam Ankon
item.description: For Zimbabwe, Wessly Madhevere took the spot of Sikandar Raza but there was no room for Graeme Cremer via Espncricinfo #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Espncricinfo

item.title: Zimbabwe Emerges as Global Leader in Domestic Tourism Growth for 2026
item.description: via Nomad Lawyer #Zimbabwe #Culture #ZimbabweCulture #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nomad Lawyer

item.title: The OK Zimbabwe Photo: What Happens When the Pavement Becomes the Marketplace?
item.description: Some photographs reveal more about an economy than official statistics. The now familiar images of informal traders operating outside an OK Zimbabwe supermarket have attracted considerable public attention. At one level, the scene appears entirely ordinary. Across Zimbabwe’s town via The Zimbabwe News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mutoko’s granite wealth leaves locals choking on dust and broken promises
item.description: IN Northern Mutoko, heavy machinery hums across the area, their dust from granite mining thickening the air. While the region produces a unique black granite worth millions of dollars on the international market, Mutoko is left choking. The dusty road networks into Mutoko North t via The Zimbabwe News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Why are fuel prices not back to normal in Zimbabwe despite global oil prices reverting to pre-war levels? @thezimbabwean
item.description: via The Zimbabwean #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwean

item.title: ‘Judge’s seat reignited my childhood dream’

item.title: Zimbabwean tycoon Strive Masiyiwa joins the board of advisors of Laurel Strategies, the global CEO advisory firm
item.description: Billionaires Africa Strive Masiyiwa, the Zimbabwean billionaire founder of Econet Group and one of Africa’s most globally connected business figures, has joined the board of advisors of Laurel Strategies, the global CEO advisory firm that counsels senior executives of Fortune 500 via The Zimbabwe News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Cash economy threatens fiscal future, warns Zimra @HeraldZimbabwe
item.description: Business Reporter THE Zimbabwe Revenue Authority (ZIMRA) has identified structural vulnerabilities undermining Zimbabwe’s fiscal foundations, including preference of cash as a mode of transaction. ZIMRA board chairperson Mr Antony Mandiwanza... via The Herald #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Herald

item.title: Zimbabwe Leads Global Domestic Tourism Surge Over USA, UK, Japan, France, Italy, Australia and Many More Countries as Consumer Behavior, Niche Markets and Economic Impact Redefine 2026 Travel Trends
